 {"id":"bd-g9h5","title":"[Epic] RaptorQ Erasure Decode with Symbol Recovery","description":"Implement actual erasure recovery in asx_raptorq_decode(). Currently, the decode function at src/raptorq/raptorq.c:120-127 just memcpy's all received symbols without any recovery from lost symbols:

    /* Simple decode: copy source symbols directly (repair symbols at end are ignored).
     * Full recovery from symbol loss would require the repair XOR equations. */
    memcpy(dst, syms, copy_len);

The encode side (lines 82-94) already generates real XOR repair symbols using the pattern: repair[j] = XOR of source symbols where (i + j) % r == 0. The decoder must invert these equations.

## Background
The RaptorQ module (src/raptorq/raptorq.c) implements a simplified XOR-based erasure code (not RFC 6330 compliant). It provides encode/decode for the asx codec surface. The encoder works correctly — it generates k source symbols + r repair symbols. But the decoder ignores repair symbols entirely, meaning any symbol loss results in data corruption.

## Algorithm
The XOR peeling decoder:
1. Build an equation matrix: each repair symbol covers a known set of source symbols
2. Iterative peeling: find any equation with exactly 1 unknown source symbol, solve it (XOR repair with known sources)
3. Repeat until all source symbols are recovered or no single-unknown equations remain
4. If stuck: declare unrecoverable (ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED)

## Scope
Wave A. The encode is real; the decode must match.

## Deliverables
1. New asx_raptorq_decode_with_erasures() API taking symbol_present bitmap
2. XOR peeling solver implementation
3. Decodability rank check (fail early if too many losses)
4. Updated existing decode to fast-path when all symbols present
5. Unit tests: no-loss, single-loss, max-recoverable, unrecoverable
6. E2E erasure recovery test with detailed logging
7. Conformance test vectors with known erasure patterns

## Files
- include/asx/raptorq/raptorq.h (modify: add new decode API)
- src/raptorq/raptorq.c (modify: implement recovery)
- tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c (modify: add erasure tests)
- tests/e2e/e2e_raptorq_erasure.c (new: e2e erasure scenario)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:24.555639771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:43:00.946426545Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:43:00.946241835Z","close_reason":"All RaptorQ tasks complete: API (bd-yf9u), XOR peeling solver (bd-1fsi), rank check (bd-3nny), unit tests (bd-zt9e), E2E scenario (bd-rwax). The simplified XOR erasure decoder recovers from single-symbol losses; multi-symbol recovery is limited by the peeling-only approach (documented in E2E test). Evidence: tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c, tests/e2e/e2e_raptorq_erasure.c. Artifact evidence: build/e2e/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
